2604.12044.title: VISTA: Validation-Informed Trajectory Adaptation via Self-Distillation
2604.12044.paper_summary: Deep learning models may converge to suboptimal solutions despite strong validation accuracy, masking an optimization failure we term Trajectory Deviation. This is because as training proceeds, models can abandon high generalization states for specific data sub-populations, thus discarding previously learned latent features without triggering classical overfitting signals. To address this problem we introduce VISTA, an online self-distillation framework that enforces consistency along the optimization trajectory. Using a validation-informed Marginal Coverage score, VISTA identifies expert anchors, which are earlier model states that retain specialized competence over distinct data regions. A coverage-weighted ensemble of these anchors is integrated online during training, regularizing the loss landscape and preserving mastered knowledge. When evaluated across multiple benchmarks, VISTA demonstrates improved robustness and generalization over standard training and prior self-distillation methods, while a lightweight implementation reduces storage overhead by 90% without performance loss.
2604.12044.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習模型可能會在強驗證準確度的情況下收斂到次優解，掩蓋了我們稱之為軌跡偏差的優化失敗。這是因為隨著訓練的進行，模型可能會放棄高泛化狀態，轉而專注於特定數據子群，從而丟棄先前學習的潛在特徵，而沒有觸發傳統的過擬合信號。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了 VISTA，一個在線自我蒸餾框架，強化了優化軌跡的一致性。利用驗證信息的邊際覆蓋分數，VISTA 識別專家錨點，這些是保留對特定數據區域專業能力的早期模型狀態。在訓練過程中，這些錨點的覆蓋加權集成在線進行，正則化損失景觀並保留已掌握的知識。在多個基準測試中評估時，VISTA 展現出比標準訓練和先前的自我蒸餾方法更好的穩健性和泛化能力，而輕量級的實現使存儲開銷減少了 90%，且不會損失性能。
2604.12044.author: Eli Corn et.al.
